name: AVAudioEngine Audio Playback
promptInjection:
=== AVAudioEngine RULES (MANDATORY) ===

ENGINE LIFECYCLE:
- Create ONE shared AVAudioEngine instance for the entire app
- Call engine.attach(playerNode) BEFORE engine.connect(playerNode, to:, format:)
- Call try engine.start() AFTER all nodes are attached and connected
- Always check engine.isRunning before scheduling buffers — if not running, call try engine.start()
- To stop: call playerNode.stop() first, then engine.stop() — never stop the engine while a node is mid-playback without stopping the node first

MULTIPLE AUDIO SOURCES (DJ apps, multi-track, etc.):
- Create a SEPARATE AVAudioPlayerNode for EACH audio source/deck/track
- Each player node must be independently attached and connected: engine.attach(playerNodeA), engine.attach(playerNodeB), engine.connect(playerNodeA, to: engine.mainMixerNode, format: format), engine.connect(playerNodeB, to: engine.mainMixerNode, format: format)
- Do NOT reuse a single player node for multiple audio files — each deck/track gets its own node
- Store player nodes in an array or dictionary keyed by deck/track ID if the number of sources is dynamic

LOADING AND PLAYING AUDIO FILES:
- Use AVAudioFile(forReading: url) to open audio files
- Read into a buffer: let buffer = AVAudioPCMBuffer(pcmFormat: file.processingFormat, frameCapacity: AVAudioFrameCount(file.length)); try file.read(into: buffer)
- Schedule the buffer: playerNode.scheduleBuffer(buffer, at: nil, options: [], completionHandler: nil)
- CRITICAL: Call playerNode.play() AFTER scheduleBuffer, not before — scheduling without play() will load the buffer but produce no sound
- The correct sequence is ALWAYS: attach → connect → engine.start() → scheduleBuffer → playerNode.play()
- To restart/replay: call playerNode.stop(), then scheduleBuffer again, then playerNode.play()

AUDIO TIME AND SCHEDULING:
- Use AVAudioTime(hostTime: mach_absolute_time()) to get current host time
- Do NOT use playerNode.nodeTime(forHostTime:) with a UInt64 — it expects AVAudioTime
- The correct conversion: let hostTime = AVAudioTime(hostTime: mach_absolute_time()); let nodeTime = playerNode.nodeTime(forNodeTime: hostTime)
- For delayed playback: let future = AVAudioTime(hostTime: mach_absolute_time() + UInt64(delaySeconds * Double(NSEC_PER_SEC))); playerNode.scheduleBuffer(buffer, at: future, options: [])
- To get current playback position: use playerNode.lastRenderTime and convert with playerNode.playerTime(forNodeTime:)

VOLUME AND CROSSFADING:
- Set volume per node: playerNodeA.volume = 0.8
- For crossfader: playerNodeA.volume = 1.0 - crossfadeValue; playerNodeB.volume = crossfadeValue (where crossfadeValue is 0.0 to 1.0)
- Master volume: engine.mainMixerNode.outputVolume = masterValue

WAVEFORM VISUALIZATION:
- Extract samples from AVAudioPCMBuffer using buffer.floatChannelData?[0] (for mono/left channel)
- Downsample for display: stride through buffer.frameLength and take max absolute value per chunk
- Store downsampled values as [Float] and render as a SwiftUI Path or Canvas drawing
- Do NOT use .foregroundStyle inside a Canvas body — use context.fill() or context.stroke() with a Color or GraphicsContext.Shading instead
- For playback position indicator: calculate progress as currentFrame / totalFrames and overlay a vertical line or highlight region on the waveform

FILE PICKER FOR AUDIO:
- Use .fileImporter(isPresented:allowedContentTypes:) with [.audio] content type
- Import UTType from UniformTypeIdentifiers
- In the completion handler, call url.startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() before reading the file and url.stopAccessingSecurityScopedResource() after loading the buffer
- If the app has multiple decks, use separate @State booleans for each file picker (showFilePickerA, showFilePickerB) — do NOT reuse one picker for both

COMMON ANTI-PATTERNS (NEVER DO THESE):
- NEVER call scheduleBuffer without calling play() afterward — the buffer loads but no sound plays
- NEVER use forHostTime: where forNodeTime: is expected — wrong argument label causes compile error
- NEVER pass UInt64 where AVAudioTime is expected — wrap with AVAudioTime(hostTime:) first
- NEVER share one AVAudioPlayerNode across multiple audio sources — each source needs its own node
- NEVER call engine.connect before engine.attach — attach must come first
- NEVER use .foregroundStyle inside a Canvas or Shape draw closure — use fill() or GraphicsContext methods
- NEVER forget url.startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() when loading files from the file picker — the read will fail silently
antiPatterns: Calling scheduleBuffer without calling play() afterward — buffer loads but no sound plays
Using forHostTime: where forNodeTime: is expected — wrong argument label causes compile error
Passing UInt64 where AVAudioTime is expected — wrap with AVAudioTime(hostTime:) first
Sharing one AVAudioPlayerNode across multiple audio sources — each source needs its own node
Calling engine.connect before engine.attach — attach must come first
Using .foregroundStyle inside a Canvas or Shape draw closure — use fill() or GraphicsContext methods
Forgetting url.startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() when loading files from the file picker
